currentState: Load-bearing practical trust. They are still sharp, dry, and unsentimental, but chapter7 makes the mission structurally dependent on both of them. Sylvanas no longer frames Jaina as support, and Jaina no longer needs Sylvanas standing over her shoulder to work at full speed.
powerDynamic: Sylvanas owns movement, capture, perimeter logic, and the final shape of the strike. Jaina owns mechanism, magical verification, and whether any human testimony becomes proof. Neither woman can close the Andorhal case alone now.

physicalDynamics.pattern: They begin with distance and control: Jaina disarmed in a holding room, Sylvanas entering with exits known and silence used as leverage.
The road fight clarifies the split in competence: Jaina touches the body, Sylvanas watches the trees; Jaina names the diagnosis, Sylvanas converts it into action.
At Millhaven and after, they learn to work the same evidence from different ends — magic stripped clean for spoor, tracks translated into warning.
Chapter5 keeps them over the same ledgers and the same night map until practical distance begins to look like shared rhythm instead of mere proximity.

Chapter6 adds a new kind of closeness: saddle-to-saddle on the ride, Jaina braced at Arthas's table while Sylvanas stands just behind her, then both women turning back to the road with the same answer in hand.

Chapter7 lets them separate without losing contact: Jaina's notes and Sylvanas's route marks meet over the same table, and even the scratch of Jaina's quill in the next room functions for Sylvanas as proof the other half of the work is still moving.

nameUsage.sylvanasCallsJaina: Jaina is now the unmarked default in working thought and planning. The mage returns only when Sylvanas wants to mark role or distance.
nameUsage.jainaCallsSylvanas: Dry specialist-to-specialist cadence. By chapter7 Jaina briefs, argues, and revises with Sylvanas as if the split lanes are already settled fact.

history.event: Their first contact comes through arrest, confiscation, and interrogation rather than diplomacy.
Sylvanas tests Jaina's warning in the field instead of handing her back to politics, and the two women survive the first road proof together.
Millhaven turns their separate methods into one usable answer about the dead moving north toward Quel'Thalas.
The living-village investigation proves they can arrive at the same conclusion from bodies and books without wasting time arguing over whose lane counts more.
Chapter5 turns that cooperation into habit as they rebuild the road pattern from depots, fragments, and route logic.
At Strahnbrad, Arthas's refusal stops being Jaina's private disappointment and becomes a problem both women read the same way. They leave the camp treating official blindness as settled fact.
Inside Andorhal they divide labor by aptitude without fighting over authority: Jaina takes the magical mechanism and Sylvanas the human cell, then they rebuild one case together that same night.

trajectory: Hostile containment -> grudging operational dependence -> shared method -> shared riding rhythm -> outward-facing alliance under strain -> split-lane partnership neither can honestly reduce anymore.
